Context: This feature measures the Audio Output from other apps on the system and lowers the Game Volume accordingly

SourceString:
This feature attenuates the Game Volume on output from other applications on your system.

<Orange>Off = External Apps will not affect the Game Volume.</>
<Orange>Communication Apps = Communication Apps (Discord, TeamSpeak, etc) will lower the Game Volume when others speak.</>
<Orange>All Apps = All Apps (Communication Apps + Music Apps, Web Browsers, etc) will lower the Game Volume.</>

SourceString:
When enabled, hold {BuildGunBuild_SnapToGuidelines} and aim to set the vertical rotation of structures with X axis rotation, such as Pipelines and Hypertubes. Aiming without holding the input will set the structure's support height.
When disabled, these controls are inverted: hold {BuildGunBuild_SnapToGuidelines} and aim to set the support height, and set vertical rotation by aiming without holding the input.
Select your desired controller type to be displayed

When enabled, hold {BuildGunBuild_SnapToGuidelines} to snap buildings to the grid.

<Orange>(Controller only)</>
When enabled, hold {PlayerMovement_Sprint} to keep sprinting. When disabled, pressing {PlayerMovement_Sprint} once will cause you to continue sprinting until you press {PlayerMovement_Sprint} again.

If enabled, aim at the powerline and hold the use button to zip along the line. If disabled, aim at the powerline and press the use button once to start and press it a second time to stop. Eliminates the need to hold the button while traveling.

If enabled, aim at the powerline and hold the use button to zip along the line. If disabled, aim at the powerline and press the use button once to start and press it a second time to stop. Eliminates the need to hold the button while traveling.

Controls how hologram rotation is applied relative to the player's location.

Controls how the hologram is rotated relative to the player. 

Available options are perpendicular (side facing the player) and parallel (front facing the player)

Direction of {PlayerController_CycleHotbarNext} and {PlayerController_CycleHotbarPrev} when cycling through hotbars. Clockwise means scrolling up will cycle clockwise. Counterclockwise means scrolling up will cycle counterclockwise.
VariableDescription:
{PlayerController_CycleHotbarNext} = text, key name
{PlayerController_CycleHotbarPrev} = text, key name

Select the desired Anti Aliasing method.

<Orange>TSR is VRAM intensive.</>

Adjust the frequency of how often the visuals of items on conveyor belts are updated.

<Orange>This setting affects CPU.</>
The distance at which items on conveyor belts will be rendered.
Enables a smoother transition between lower-detail models.

Adjust the render distance of factory buildings. 

<Orange>Zero means always visible, two means rendered twice as far.</>
Uses AI to create additional frames between rendered ones, boosting framerate and smoothing motion.
Controls the overall brightness in the game.

Lowers the quality of the depth buffer. 

<Orange>May have a performance impact on some systems.</>
Changes the mood in enclosed spaces with Lumen.

The percentage of the resolution the game will be rendered at.

<Orange>Example: 50% means the game will be rendered at half the resolution, but scaled up to fit the entire window size.</>
<Orange>When running with TSR, the final image will be scaled up to full resolution.</>

Select Upscaling Method.

<Orange>TSR is VRAM intensive.</>

<Orange>Performance (performance over quality, some rendering artifacts)</>
<Orange>Balanced (balance between performance and quality)</>
<Orange>Quality (quality over performance)</>
<Orange>No Downscaling (Only anti aliasing, not recommended!)</>
Enables vertical synchronization. This can prevent screen tearing.

Enabling Virtual Shadows is only recommended for high-end machines. 

<Orange>This feature is experimental.</>
Enabling volumetric clouds will make the clouds appear more realistic, but may impact performance.

The world is made up of individual tiles. This setting controls how fast the world (tiles) will load and unload. Lower settings may reduce level streaming hitches, but will reduce long-distance rendering quality.
Sets the field of view. A wider field of view increases the visible range of the environment.
Adjust the scale of equipment shown in first person.
Maintain Y Axis FOV Aspect Ratio
Controls which axis is dominant when adjusting for FOV. Experimenting with this is recommended when playing in ultrawide resolutions.
Displays the game in Fullscreen mode.

Selects which low level graphics API to use. DirectX 12 is tested and considered stable. Vulkan is less tested but may offer a performance increase. DirectX 11 is deprecated but can still be used.

<Orange>Changing this value requires you to restart the game for the change to take effect.</>

Current Graphics API is forced either because your hardware/OS doesn't support the desired API or it's being forced by a launch option. 
If you want to use another Graphics API try overriding with launch options  "-dx11", "-d3d11",  "-dx12", "-d3d12" or "-vulkan"

DX11 is deprecated and is not actively supported by the developers. But it can still be used.

Changing this value requires you to restart the game for the change to take effect.

Vulkan is less tested and stable than DirectX 12 but may offer a performance increase on certain systems. 

Changing this value requires you to restart the game for the change to take effect.
Sets the maximum frames per second.
Changes the resolution. Resolution cannot be changed while in Windowed Fullscreen.
Context: Windowed Fullscreen refers to the Fullscreen setting of the same name (String key: Video/Display/Fullscreen/Values/WindowedFullscreen).

SourceString:
The global graphics quality setting. 

<Orange>In order to enable 'Balanced' mode, a 120Hz screen is required and the 120Hz 'refresh rate' needs to be activated in your console settings.</>
<Orange>Variable Refresh Rate (VRR) is not supported. If you’re having issues, for the best experience, please turn off VRR on your TV and your Console.</>

The global graphics quality setting. 

<Orange>Changes the values of other settings.</>

Runs a benchmark test and sets the graphics options to a preset based on the results.

<Orange>This may take a few seconds.</>
Turn on to prevent jagged lines along edges.
Sets the quality of clouds in the game.

Controls how quickly foliage will be loaded in.

<Orange>Use the Cinematic setting at your own risk.</>
Sets the quality of foliage/vegetation in the game.
A higher setting means more lights will be rendered onscreen at once, and a lower setting means fewer lights will be rendered at once.
Enable to see motion blur when moving the camera in the game.
Affects post processing effects such as motion blur, fog, etc.
Distance that far shadows will trace, lower values can cause gaps in the shadows. High values will cost more performance on the GPU.

SourceString:
Set the rate at which the weather changes.

<Orange>Default</>
<Orange>Short</>
<Orange>Long</>
<Orange>Very Long</>

<Orange>Dry:</> Overcast, wind and clear weather.
<Orange>Wet:</> Rain, overcast and clear weather. Some thunder.
<Orange>The Great MASSAGE-2 (AB)b:</> Wind and rain, with a touch of overcast and thunderstorms.
<Orange>Clear:</> Clear weather with a touch of wind, rain, and thunder.
<Orange>Raining Kittens and Puppies:</> Chance of rain and windy weather. Low chance of thunder and clear weather.
<Orange>Extreme:</> Chance of rain, thunderstorms, and wind. Low chance of clear weather.
